Metadata.CommitMsg: commit finale : déploiement Ginflix sur Kubernetes (Kind + Cilium + Vault + HPA + Caddy)
Metadata.Author: Laury Kibamba <laury.kibamba@telecom-paris.fr>

Misconfigurations.Title: Can elevate its own privileges
Misconfigurations.Description: A program inside the container can elevate its own privileges and run as root, which might give the program control over the container and node.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'backend' of Deployment 'backend' should set 'securityContext.allowPrivilegeEscalation' to false

Misconfigurations.Title: Default capabilities: some containers do not drop all
Misconfigurations.Description: The container should drop all default capabilities and add only those that are needed for its execution.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'backend' of Deployment 'backend' should add 'ALL' to 'securityContext.capabilities.drop'

Misconfigurations.Title: Default capabilities: some containers do not drop any
Misconfigurations.Description: Security best practices require containers to run with minimal required capabilities.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'backend' of 'deployment' 'backend' in 'ginflix' namespace should set securityContext.capabilities.drop
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Specify at least one unneeded capability in 'containers[].securityContext.capabilities.drop'

Misconfigurations.Title: Runs as root user
Misconfigurations.Description: Force the running image to run as a non-root user to ensure least privileges.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'backend' of Deployment 'backend' should set 'securityContext.runAsNonRoot' to true

Misconfigurations.Title: Image tag ":latest" used
Misconfigurations.Description: It is best to avoid using the ':latest' image tag when deploying containers in production. Doing so makes it hard to track which version of the image is running, and hard to roll back the version.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'backend' of Deployment 'backend' should specify an image tag
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Use a specific container image tag that is not 'latest'.

Misconfigurations.Title: Root file system is not read-only
Misconfigurations.Description: An immutable root file system prevents applications from writing to their local disk. This can limit intrusions, as attackers will not be able to tamper with the file system or write foreign executables to disk.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'backend' of Deployment 'backend' should set 'securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem' to true

Misconfigurations.Title: Runs with UID <= 10000
Misconfigurations.Description: Force the container to run with user ID > 10000 to avoid conflicts with the host’s user table.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'backend' of Deployment 'backend' should set 'securityContext.runAsUser' > 10000

Misconfigurations.Title: Runs with GID <= 10000
Misconfigurations.Description: Force the container to run with group ID > 10000 to avoid conflicts with the host’s user table.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'backend' of Deployment 'backend' should set 'securityContext.runAsGroup' > 10000

Misconfigurations.Title: Runtime/Default Seccomp profile not set
Misconfigurations.Description: According to pod security standard 'Seccomp', the RuntimeDefault seccomp profile must be required, or allow specific additional profiles.
Misconfigurations.Message: Either Pod or Container should set 'securityContext.seccompProfile.type' to 'RuntimeDefault'

Misconfigurations.Title: Seccomp policies disabled
Misconfigurations.Description: A program inside the container can bypass Seccomp protection policies.
Misconfigurations.Message: container "backend" of deployment "backend" in "ginflix" namespace should specify a seccomp profile
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Specify seccomp either by annotation or by seccomp profile type having allowed values as per pod security standards

Misconfigurations.Title: Container capabilities must only include NET_BIND_SERVICE
Misconfigurations.Description: Containers must drop ALL capabilities, and are only permitted to add back the NET_BIND_SERVICE capability.
Misconfigurations.Message: container should drop all
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set 'spec.containers[*].securityContext.capabilities.drop' to 'ALL' and only add 'NET_BIND_SERVICE' to 'spec.containers[*].securityContext.capabilities.add'.

Misconfigurations.Title: Default security context configured
Misconfigurations.Description: Security context controls the allocation of security parameters for the pod/container/volume, ensuring the appropriate level of protection. Relying on default security context may expose vulnerabilities to potential attacks that rely on privileged access.
Misconfigurations.Message: container backend in ginflix namespace is using the default security context
Misconfigurations.Resolution: To enhance security, it is strongly recommended not to rely on the default security context. Instead, it is advisable to explicitly define the required security parameters (such as runAsNonRoot, capabilities, readOnlyRootFilesystem, etc.) within the security context.

Misconfigurations.Title: Default security context configured
Misconfigurations.Description: Security context controls the allocation of security parameters for the pod/container/volume, ensuring the appropriate level of protection. Relying on default security context may expose vulnerabilities to potential attacks that rely on privileged access.
Misconfigurations.Message: deployment backend in ginflix namespace is using the default security context, which allows root privileges
Misconfigurations.Resolution: To enhance security, it is strongly recommended not to rely on the default security context. Instead, it is advisable to explicitly define the required security parameters (such as runAsNonRoot, capabilities, readOnlyRootFilesystem, etc.) within the security context.

Misconfigurations.Title: Restrict container images to trusted registries
Misconfigurations.Description: Ensure that all containers use images only from trusted registry domains.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container backend in deployment backend (namespace: ginflix) uses an image from an untrusted registry.

Misconfigurations.Title: ConfigMap with sensitive content
Misconfigurations.Description: Storing sensitive content such as usernames and email addresses in configMaps is unsafe
Misconfigurations.Message: ConfigMap 'ginflix-config' in 'ginflix' namespace stores sensitive contents in key(s) or value(s) '{"KEYCLOAK_CLIENT_ID", "KEYCLOAK_HOST", "KEYCLOAK_REALM"}'
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Remove sensitive content from configMap data value

Misconfigurations.Title: Can elevate its own privileges
Misconfigurations.Description: A program inside the container can elevate its own privileges and run as root, which might give the program control over the container and node.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'mongo' of Deployment 'database' should set 'securityContext.allowPrivilegeEscalation' to false

Misconfigurations.Title: Default capabilities: some containers do not drop all
Misconfigurations.Description: The container should drop all default capabilities and add only those that are needed for its execution.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'mongo' of Deployment 'database' should add 'ALL' to 'securityContext.capabilities.drop'

Misconfigurations.Title: Default capabilities: some containers do not drop any
Misconfigurations.Description: Security best practices require containers to run with minimal required capabilities.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'mongo' of 'deployment' 'database' in 'ginflix' namespace should set securityContext.capabilities.drop
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Specify at least one unneeded capability in 'containers[].securityContext.capabilities.drop'

Misconfigurations.Title: CPU not limited
Misconfigurations.Description: Enforcing CPU limits prevents DoS via resource exhaustion.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'mongo' of Deployment 'database' should set 'resources.limits.cpu'

Misconfigurations.Title: Runs as root user
Misconfigurations.Description: Force the running image to run as a non-root user to ensure least privileges.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'mongo' of Deployment 'database' should set 'securityContext.runAsNonRoot' to true

Misconfigurations.Title: Root file system is not read-only
Misconfigurations.Description: An immutable root file system prevents applications from writing to their local disk. This can limit intrusions, as attackers will not be able to tamper with the file system or write foreign executables to disk.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'mongo' of Deployment 'database' should set 'securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem' to true

Misconfigurations.Title: CPU requests not specified
Misconfigurations.Description: When containers have resource requests specified, the scheduler can make better decisions about which nodes to place pods on, and how to deal with resource contention.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'mongo' of Deployment 'database' should set 'resources.requests.cpu'

Misconfigurations.Title: Memory requests not specified
Misconfigurations.Description: When containers have memory requests specified, the scheduler can make better decisions about which nodes to place pods on, and how to deal with resource contention.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'mongo' of Deployment 'database' should set 'resources.requests.memory'

Misconfigurations.Title: Memory not limited
Misconfigurations.Description: Enforcing memory limits prevents DoS via resource exhaustion.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'mongo' of Deployment 'database' should set 'resources.limits.memory'

Misconfigurations.Title: Runs with UID <= 10000
Misconfigurations.Description: Force the container to run with user ID > 10000 to avoid conflicts with the host’s user table.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'mongo' of Deployment 'database' should set 'securityContext.runAsUser' > 10000

Misconfigurations.Title: Runs with GID <= 10000
Misconfigurations.Description: Force the container to run with group ID > 10000 to avoid conflicts with the host’s user table.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'mongo' of Deployment 'database' should set 'securityContext.runAsGroup' > 10000

Misconfigurations.Title: Runtime/Default Seccomp profile not set
Misconfigurations.Description: According to pod security standard 'Seccomp', the RuntimeDefault seccomp profile must be required, or allow specific additional profiles.
Misconfigurations.Message: Either Pod or Container should set 'securityContext.seccompProfile.type' to 'RuntimeDefault'

Misconfigurations.Title: Seccomp policies disabled
Misconfigurations.Description: A program inside the container can bypass Seccomp protection policies.
Misconfigurations.Message: container "mongo" of deployment "database" in "ginflix" namespace should specify a seccomp profile
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Specify seccomp either by annotation or by seccomp profile type having allowed values as per pod security standards

Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set 'spec.containers[*].securityContext.capabilities.drop' to 'ALL' and only add 'NET_BIND_SERVICE' to 'spec.containers[*].securityContext.capabilities.add'.

Misconfigurations.Description: Security context controls the allocation of security parameters for the pod/container/volume, ensuring the appropriate level of protection. Relying on default security context may expose vulnerabilities to potential attacks that rely on privileged access.
Misconfigurations.Message: container database in ginflix namespace is using the default security context
Misconfigurations.Resolution: To enhance security, it is strongly recommended not to rely on the default security context. Instead, it is advisable to explicitly define the required security parameters (such as runAsNonRoot, capabilities, readOnlyRootFilesystem, etc.) within the security context.

Misconfigurations.Description: A program inside the container can elevate its own privileges and run as root, which might give the program control over the container and node.

Misconfigurations.Title: Container capabilities must only include NET_BIND_SERVICE
Misconfigurations.Description: Containers must drop ALL capabilities, and are only permitted to add back the NET_BIND_SERVICE capability.
Misconfigurations.Message: container should drop all
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set 'spec.containers[*].securityContext.capabilities.drop' to 'ALL' and only add 'NET_BIND_SERVICE' to 'spec.containers[*].securityContext.capabilities.add'.

Misconfigurations.Title: Prevent binding to privileged ports
Misconfigurations.Description: The ports which are lower than 1024 receive and transmit various sensitive and privileged data. Allowing containers to use them can bring serious implications.
Misconfigurations.Message: deployment frontend-admin in ginflix namespace should not set spec.template.spec.containers.ports.containerPort to less than 1024
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Do not map the container ports to privileged host ports when starting a container.

Misconfigurations.Title: Default security context configured
Misconfigurations.Description: Security context controls the allocation of security parameters for the pod/container/volume, ensuring the appropriate level of protection. Relying on default security context may expose vulnerabilities to potential attacks that rely on privileged access.
Misconfigurations.Message: container frontend-admin in ginflix namespace is using the default security context
Misconfigurations.Resolution: To enhance security, it is strongly recommended not to rely on the default security context. Instead, it is advisable to explicitly define the required security parameters (such as runAsNonRoot, capabilities, readOnlyRootFilesystem, etc.) within the security context.

Misconfigurations.Message: Deployment 'metrics-server' should not be set with 'kube-system' namespace
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Deploy the user resources into a designated namespace which is not kube-system.

Misconfigurations.Title: Restrict container images to trusted registries
Misconfigurations.Description: Ensure that all containers use images only from trusted registry domains.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container metrics-server in deployment metrics-server (namespace: kube-system) uses an image from an untrusted registry.

Misconfigurations.Title: User resources should not be placed in kube-system namespace
Misconfigurations.Description: ensure that user resources are not placed in kube-system namespace
Misconfigurations.Message: Service 'metrics-server' should not be set with 'kube-system' namespace
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Deploy the user resources into a designated namespace which is not kube-system.

Misconfigurations.Title: Can elevate its own privileges
Misconfigurations.Description: A program inside the container can elevate its own privileges and run as root, which might give the program control over the container and node.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'streamer' of Deployment 'streamer' should set 'securityContext.allowPrivilegeEscalation' to false

Misconfigurations.Title: Default capabilities: some containers do not drop all
Misconfigurations.Description: The container should drop all default capabilities and add only those that are needed for its execution.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'streamer' of Deployment 'streamer' should add 'ALL' to 'securityContext.capabilities.drop'

Misconfigurations.Title: Default capabilities: some containers do not drop any
Misconfigurations.Description: Security best practices require containers to run with minimal required capabilities.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'streamer' of 'deployment' 'streamer' in 'ginflix' namespace should set securityContext.capabilities.drop
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Specify at least one unneeded capability in 'containers[].securityContext.capabilities.drop'

Misconfigurations.Title: Runs as root user
Misconfigurations.Description: Force the running image to run as a non-root user to ensure least privileges.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'streamer' of Deployment 'streamer' should set 'securityContext.runAsNonRoot' to true

Misconfigurations.Title: Image tag ":latest" used
Misconfigurations.Description: It is best to avoid using the ':latest' image tag when deploying containers in production. Doing so makes it hard to track which version of the image is running, and hard to roll back the version.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'streamer' of Deployment 'streamer' should specify an image tag
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Use a specific container image tag that is not 'latest'.

Misconfigurations.Title: Root file system is not read-only
Misconfigurations.Description: An immutable root file system prevents applications from writing to their local disk. This can limit intrusions, as attackers will not be able to tamper with the file system or write foreign executables to disk.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'streamer' of Deployment 'streamer' should set 'securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem' to true

Misconfigurations.Title: Runs with UID <= 10000
Misconfigurations.Description: Force the container to run with user ID > 10000 to avoid conflicts with the host’s user table.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'streamer' of Deployment 'streamer' should set 'securityContext.runAsUser' > 10000

Misconfigurations.Title: Runs with GID <= 10000
Misconfigurations.Description: Force the container to run with group ID > 10000 to avoid conflicts with the host’s user table.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'streamer' of Deployment 'streamer' should set 'securityContext.runAsGroup' > 10000

Misconfigurations.Title: Runtime/Default Seccomp profile not set
Misconfigurations.Description: According to pod security standard 'Seccomp', the RuntimeDefault seccomp profile must be required, or allow specific additional profiles.
Misconfigurations.Message: Either Pod or Container should set 'securityContext.seccompProfile.type' to 'RuntimeDefault'

Misconfigurations.Title: Seccomp policies disabled
Misconfigurations.Description: A program inside the container can bypass Seccomp protection policies.
Misconfigurations.Message: container "streamer" of deployment "streamer" in "ginflix" namespace should specify a seccomp profile
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Specify seccomp either by annotation or by seccomp profile type having allowed values as per pod security standards

Misconfigurations.Title: Container capabilities must only include NET_BIND_SERVICE
Misconfigurations.Description: Containers must drop ALL capabilities, and are only permitted to add back the NET_BIND_SERVICE capability.
Misconfigurations.Message: container should drop all
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Set 'spec.containers[*].securityContext.capabilities.drop' to 'ALL' and only add 'NET_BIND_SERVICE' to 'spec.containers[*].securityContext.capabilities.add'.

Misconfigurations.Title: Default security context configured
Misconfigurations.Description: Security context controls the allocation of security parameters for the pod/container/volume, ensuring the appropriate level of protection. Relying on default security context may expose vulnerabilities to potential attacks that rely on privileged access.
Misconfigurations.Message: container streamer in ginflix namespace is using the default security context
Misconfigurations.Resolution: To enhance security, it is strongly recommended not to rely on the default security context. Instead, it is advisable to explicitly define the required security parameters (such as runAsNonRoot, capabilities, readOnlyRootFilesystem, etc.) within the security context.

Misconfigurations.Title: Default security context configured
Misconfigurations.Description: Security context controls the allocation of security parameters for the pod/container/volume, ensuring the appropriate level of protection. Relying on default security context may expose vulnerabilities to potential attacks that rely on privileged access.
Misconfigurations.Message: deployment streamer in ginflix namespace is using the default security context, which allows root privileges
Misconfigurations.Resolution: To enhance security, it is strongly recommended not to rely on the default security context. Instead, it is advisable to explicitly define the required security parameters (such as runAsNonRoot, capabilities, readOnlyRootFilesystem, etc.) within the security context.

Misconfigurations.Title: Restrict container images to trusted registries
Misconfigurations.Description: Ensure that all containers use images only from trusted registry domains.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container streamer in deployment streamer (namespace: ginflix) uses an image from an untrusted registry.
